title: Decide: retire internal/agent + internal/runrecord or accept as shared packages
description:
Surfaced during epic vuq (tick 0u6): the wwv verdict said to drop internal/agent and internal/runrecord 'once importer-free', but they have live non-runner importers: (1) internal/context/generator.go - tk context generates epic context docs by running an AI agent through the agent.Agent abstraction; (2) internal/migrate/runrecord.go - tk migrate migrates historical run records; (3) internal/ticks/types.go Task.Run field; (4) board verification badges - internal/tickboard/server reads runrecord for /api/records/:tickId and kanban verification status (under Claude orchestration nothing writes new run records, so badges only ever show historical data).

Options:
A) Accept both packages as shared data/abstraction surface; do nothing (status quo after vuq).
B) Retire them fully: rework tk context off the agent abstraction (or delete tk context - is it used?), drop runrecord migration from tk migrate, remove Task.Run, drop verification badges from the board. Bigger; touches product behavior.
C) Middle: keep runrecord as a read-only historical format; delete internal/agent by reworking tk context only.

Record verdict via tk note + approve. Relevant code: internal/context/generator.go, internal/migrate/runrecord.go, internal/tickboard/server/server.go handleRecords/computeVerificationStatus.
notes: 2026-06-10 21:59 - [human] decision: B (retire fully). Delete internal/agent, internal/runrecord, internal/context; delete tk context (read path included - nothing generates context docs anymore); drop run-record migration from tk migrate (keep the rest); remove board verification badges, Attempts tab, /api/records and /api/context; remove Task.Run from ticks types. Historical .tick/logs data stays on disk, just unread.
acceptance_criteria: Verdict recorded in notes and tick approved
